_comment: VS Code snippets for Tensor Omics trajectory contribution analysis (TCA) functions

body: call get_vec_across_samples(trajectories, n_factors, n_samples, n_timepoints, i_factor, i_timepoint, result_vector, ierr)
call get_vec_across_timepoints(trajectories, n_factors, n_samples, n_timepoints, i_factor, i_sample, result_vector, ierr)
call trajectory_contribution(factor, dependent, n_timepoints, mode, contribution, ierr)
call spike_contribution(factor, dependent, n_timepoints, mode, contribution, ierr)
Compute trajectory-level contribution between two vectors.
factor (np.ndarray): 1D array of shape (n_timepoints,) — independent variable
dependent (np.ndarray): 1D array of shape (n_timepoints,) — dependent variable
mode (int): 1 for cosine similarity, 2 for angle (acos)
Python set up tox_trajectory_contributio.description: Sets up the python function to call tox_trajectory_contribution

body: Compute spike-wise contribution between two vectors using directional or angular mode.
factor (np.ndarray): 1D array of shape (n_timepoints,) representing the independent variable.
dependent (np.ndarray): 1D array of shape (n_timepoints,) representing the dependent variable.
mode (int): Mode of contribution (1 = Normal, 2 = RAP).
np.ndarray: 1D array of shape (n_timepoints,) with contribution values per timepoint.
raise ValueError("factor and dependent must have the same shape")
Python set up tox_spike_contribution.description: Sets up the python function to call tox_spike_contribution

body: call compute_baselines_factor_dependent(n_timepoints, factor, dependent, mode, factor_baseline, dependent_baseline, ierr)
Call to compute_baselines_factor_depende.description: Compute scalar baselines for factor and dependent time series

body: Compute scalar baselines for a factor and dependent variable.
Calculates baseline values for two time series based on the specified mode:
factor (np.ndarray): 1D array of shape (n_timepoints,) — factor time series
dependent (np.ndarray): 1D array of shape (n_timepoints,) — dependent time series
- 'baseline_dependent': float, baseline value for dependent variable
ValueError: If factor and dependent have different lengths
RuntimeError: If error occurs during computation (invalid mode, etc.)
raise ValueError("factor and dependent must have the same shape")
raise ValueError("factor and dependent must be 1D arrays")
# Setup C wrapper with proper type annotations
# Call Fortran routine via C wrapper
Python set up tox_compute_baselines_fact.description: Sets up the python function to call tox_compute_baselines_factor_dependent

Call to calc_contributions.description: Compute whole trajectory contribution and local spike contributions for given independent and dependent

Call to calc_contributions_alloc.description: Compute whole trajectory contribution and local spike contributions for given independent and dependent

body: Wrapper for calc_contributions_c: calculates spike and integrated contributions using internal allocation.
trajectories (np.ndarray): 3D array of shape (n_factors, n_samples, n_timepoints)
i_factor (int): 0-based index of the independent variable
dependent_idx (int): 0-based index of the dependent variable
def tox_calc_contributions_expert(trajectories, i_factor, dependent_idx, mode, temp_factor_vector, temp_dependent_vector):
Wrapper for calc_contributions_expert_c: calculates spike and integrated contributions using caller-provided buffers.
trajectories (np.ndarray): 3D array of shape (n_factors, n_samples, n_timepoints)
i_factor (int): 0-based index of the independent variable
dependent_idx (int): 0-based index of the dependent variable
! Compute EDF (convenience wrapper) - sorts internally and computes EDF
call compute_edf_alloc(values, n_values, unique_values, cdf_values, n_unique, ierr)
Call to compute_edf_alloc.description: Invoke compute_edf_alloc which sorts internally and computes the empirical distribution function

body: ! Compute empirical distribution function (EDF) - cumulative proportion of values ≤ each unique data point
call compute_edf(values, n_values, perm, unique_values, cdf_values, n_unique, ierr)
Call to compute_edf.description: Invoke compute_edf to obtain sorted unique values and their cumulative frequencies

body: Compute empirical distribution function (EDF) for an array of values.
This wrapper calls the C ABI `compute_edf_c` which sorts internally and returns
the unique values and their cumulative distribution (CDF).
Python set up compute_edf.description: Python setup for compute_edf_c: convenience wrapper that returns unique values and CDF

body: Compute empirical distribution function (EDF) and return the permutation, unique values and CDF.
This wrapper calls the C ABI `compute_edf_expert_c` which produces a permutation array (perm),
the unique sorted values and their cumulative distribution (CDF).
Python set up compute_edf_expert level.description: Python setup for compute_edf_expert_c: returns permutation, unique values and CDF

Call to process trajectories.description: Call to process entire trajectories pipeline, uses allocations internally

Call to process flattened trajectories.description: Process trajectories with one percentile per timepoint for spike contributions

body: def tox_process_trajectories(trajectories, factor_mask, dependent_idx, mode, percentile):
Wrapper for process_trajectories_alloc_C: processes multiple trajectories with per-timepoint percentiles.
trajectories (np.ndarray): 3D array of shape (n_factors, n_samples, n_timepoints)
factor_mask (np.ndarray): 1D boolean array of shape (n_factors,) indicating which factors to process
dependent_idx (int): 1-based index of the dependent variable
percentile (float): Percentile value for threshold calculation (0.0-100.0)
'thresholds_spike': np.ndarray,       # Thresholds for spike contributions [n_timepoints, n_processed_factors]
'outliers_integrated': np.ndarray,    # Outliers for integrated contributions [n_samples, n_processed_factors]
'outliers_spike': np.ndarray          # Outliers for spike contributions [n_timepoints, n_samples, n_processed_factors]
# Convert boolean mask to integer array for C
# Calculate number of processed factors (excluding dependent_idx)
raise ValueError("No factors to process after excluding dependent variable")
spike_contribs = np.zeros((n_timepoints, n_samples, n_processed), dtype=np.float64, order="F")
outliers_spike = np.zeros((n_timepoints, n_samples, n_processed), dtype=np.int32, order='F')
# Convert integer outliers back to boolean for Python
Python set up tox_process_trajectories.description: Sets up the python function to call tox_process_trajectories

body: Wrapper for process_trajectories_flat_alloc_C: processes trajectories with global percentile for spike contributions.
trajectories (np.ndarray): 3D array of shape (n_factors, n_samples, n_timepoints)
factor_mask (np.ndarray): 1D boolean array of shape (n_factors,) indicating which factors to process
dependent_idx (int): 1-based index of the dependent variable
percentile (float): Percentile value for threshold calculation (0.0-100.0)
'outliers_integrated': np.ndarray,    # Outliers for integrated contributions [n_samples, n_processed_factors]
'outliers_spike': np.ndarray          # Outliers for spike contributions [n_timepoints, n_samples, n_processed_factors]
# Convert boolean mask to integer array for C
# Calculate number of processed factors (excluding dependent_idx)
raise ValueError("No factors to process after excluding dependent variable")
spike_contribs = np.empty((n_timepoints, n_samples, n_processed), dtype=np.float64, order="F")
thresholds_spike = np.empty(n_processed, dtype=np.float64, order='F')  # 1D for flat version
outliers_spike = np.empty((n_timepoints, n_samples, n_processed), dtype=np.int32, order='F')
# Convert integer outliers back to boolean for Python
Python set up tox_process_trajectories_f.description: Sets up the python function to call tox_process_trajectories_flat

body: def calc_spike_thresholds_expert(spike_contribs: np.ndarray, percentile_val: float, permutation: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
Calculate empirical thresholds for spike contributions using pre-sorted permutations
spike_contribs: 2D array of spike contributions [n_timepoints, n_samples]
(each column contains sorted indices for a timepoint) in Fortran order
thresholds: 1D array of thresholds for each timepoint [n_timepoints] (read-only)
# Ensure correct data types and memory layout
raise ValueError(f"permutation shape {permutation.shape} doesn't match expected (n_samples, n_timepoints) = ({n_samples}, {n_timepoints})")
raise ValueError("percentile_val must be between 0.0 and 100.0")
# Setup C wrapper - using expert_C version with permutations

body: def detect_outliers_integrated(contributions: np.ndarray, threshold: float) -> np.ndarray:
contributions: 1D array of integrated contributions [n_samples] in Fortran order
threshold: Scalar threshold value for outlier detection
outlier_mask: 1D boolean array indicating outlier samples [n_samples] (read-only)
# Ensure correct data types and memory layout
outlier_mask = np.zeros(n_samples, dtype=np.int32, order='F')  # C int for logical
# Setup C wrapper - using expert_C version
# Convert C int (0/1) to Python boolean

outlier_mask: 2D boolean array indicating outliers [n_timepoints, n_samples] (read-only)
# Ensure correct data types and memory layout
raise ValueError(f"thresholds size {thresholds.size} doesn't match n_timepoints {n_timepoints}")
outlier_mask = np.zeros((n_timepoints, n_samples), dtype=np.int32, order='F')  # C int for logical
